description: Per-level storage details for debugging. Deep dive into compaction, throttle, deduplication, and compression internals. Use this dashboard for troubleshooting storage performance issues.

panels.description:
Bytes written to each LSM level during compaction.

Level 0 receives data from memtable flushes.
Levels 1-6 receive data from compaction of the level above.

Higher write activity at upper levels indicates more compaction churn.
panels.title: Per-Level Bytes Written

panels.description:
Write amplification ratio for each LSM level.

Level 0: Usually 1.0 (direct memtable flush)
Levels 1-6: Typically 2-10x depending on data overlap

High write amplification at a specific level indicates heavy compaction activity there.
panels.title: Per-Level Write Amplification

panels.description:
Compaction frequency per level showing how often each level undergoes compaction.

Higher rates at L0/L1 are normal.
Rates should decrease at lower levels.
panels.title: Per-Level Compaction Rate

panels.description:
Heatmap showing compaction activity intensity by level over time.

Brighter colors indicate more compaction I/O at that level.
panels.title: Compaction Activity Heatmap

panels.description:
Number of input and output tables involved in compaction operations.

Input tables: Number of SSTables being merged
Output tables: Number of SSTables produced

High input counts with low output counts indicate effective compaction (merging many into few).
panels.title: Compaction Tables I/O

panels.description:
Distribution of compaction operation durations.

Short compactions (<1s): Typical L0-L1 compactions
Medium compactions (1-10s): Full level compactions
Long compactions (>10s): Major compactions, may impact performance
panels.title: Compaction Duration Percentiles

panels.description:
Compaction throttle ratio overlaid with P99 query latency.

When throttle ratio drops, P99 latency should improve.
If both are high simultaneously, the system is under heavy load.
panels.title: Throttle Ratio vs P99 Latency

panels.description:
Compaction pending bytes driving throttle decisions.

Throttle activates when pending bytes exceeds threshold.
Higher pending bytes = more aggressive throttling.
panels.title: Compaction Pending Bytes (Throttle Driver)

panels.description:
Memory usage of the deduplication index.

The dedup index is bounded per-level to prevent memory explosion.
LRU eviction kicks in when memory limit is reached.
panels.title: Dedup Index Memory Usage

panels.description:
LRU evictions from the deduplication index.

Evictions occur when index memory limit is reached.
High eviction rate may indicate memory limit too low for workload.
panels.title: Dedup LRU Evictions/sec

panels.description:
Compression throughput showing bytes in and bytes out over time.

The gap between lines represents compression savings.
panels.title: Compression Throughput

panels.description:
Compression savings rate over time.

Shows how many bytes per second are being saved by compression.
panels.title: Compression Savings Rate

panels.description:
Block read and write operations per second.

Block reads occur during queries and compaction.
Block writes occur during compaction and flushes.
panels.title: Block I/O Operations

panels.description:
Block cache hit ratio.

Higher hit ratio = more reads served from memory.
Low hit ratio indicates cache may be undersized or access patterns are unfriendly.
panels.title: Block Cache Hit Ratio

panels.description:
Write rate metrics from different observability windows.

Shows 1-minute, 5-minute, and 1-hour rolling averages.
Useful for understanding write pattern variability.
panels.title: Write Rate (Rolling Windows)

title: ArcherDB Storage - Deep Dive
